question_text: A student has two identical cubes made of different materials. Cube A has a mass of 270 g and a volume of 30 cm³. Cube B has a mass of 540 g and a volume of 60 cm³. Which statement correctly compares the densities of the two cubes?

options.label: A
options.text: Cube A has a higher density because it has a smaller volume

options.label: B
options.text: Both cubes have the same density of 9 g/cm³

options.label: C
options.text: Cube B has a higher density because it has a greater mass

options.label: D
options.text: Cube A has a higher density because mass and volume are both smaller

explanation: To compare densities, we must calculate them using density = mass ÷ volume. Cube A: 270 ÷ 30 = 9 g/cm³. Cube B: 540 ÷ 60 = 9 g/cm³. Both have the same density despite different masses and volumes, showing that density is a property independent of the amount of material.
